main.q: Which of these is the largest mammal that has ever lived?
main.lesson: Blue whales reach up to 30 m and 200 tonnes — larger than any dinosaur fossil ever recovered. They feed almost entirely on krill.

main.q: Honeybees communicate the direction and distance of nectar sources using…
main.lesson: Karl von Frisch decoded the waggle dance in the 1940s — the angle relative to gravity tells direction, the duration tells distance.

main.q: Which of these biomes has the highest biodiversity per hectare?
main.lesson: Coral reefs cover less than 1% of the ocean floor but host roughly 25% of all marine species.

main.q: What's the deepest part of the world's oceans?
main.lesson: The Mariana Trench's Challenger Deep is around 11,000 m below sea level — deeper than Everest is tall.

main.q: Trees in a forest can share resources via…
main.lesson: Symbiotic root fungi (the 'wood-wide web') let trees exchange carbon, nitrogen, and even chemical signals across species.

bonus.q: Which of these animals is technically a fish but breathes air?
bonus.lesson: Lungfish have functioning lungs and survive dry seasons by aestivating in mud cocoons.

bonus.q: What proportion of Earth's oxygen is produced by ocean plankton?
bonus.lesson: Phytoplankton — drifting marine algae — generate roughly half the oxygen we breathe, more than all forests combined.
